data.result_being_validated:
```text
1. Analyze Job Description: (Completed in task0)
   - Extract role requirements and evaluation criteria.
2. Analyze Candidate Resume: (Completed in task1)
   - Identify skills, experiences, and fit.
3. Conduct Technical Interview:
   a. Screening Round:
      i. Question 1: "Describe your experience with Python and Django in building RESTful APIs."
         - Competency Tested: Technical Skills (Python, Django, RESTful APIs)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Specific projects, technologies used, outcomes.
         - Follow-up Probes: "Can you describe a particularly challenging API you designed?", "What tools do you use for API testing?"
      ii. Question 2: "Explain your experience with React and Node.js, focusing on performance optimization techniques you've used."
         - Competency Tested: Technical Skills (React, Node.js, Performance Optimization)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Specific examples of optimizations, tools used, performance metrics.
         - Follow-up Probes: "How do you profile React applications for performance?", "What are common Node.js performance bottlenecks and how do you address them?"
      iii. Question 3: "Discuss your experience with AWS services, particularly EC2, S3, and Lambda."
         - Competency Tested: Technical Skills (AWS, Cloud Deployment)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Specific use cases, architectures implemented, cost optimization strategies.
         - Follow-up Probes: "Can you describe a serverless architecture you've implemented using Lambda?", "How do you secure S3 buckets?", "What are the differences between EC2 instance types and when would you use each?"
   b. Technical Round:
      i. Question 1: "Describe your experience with CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Docker."
         - Competency Tested: Technical Skills (CI/CD, DevOps)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Specific implementations, automation strategies, reduction in deployment errors.
         - Follow-up Probes: "What are the key components of your CI/CD pipeline?", "How do you handle rollbacks in your CI/CD pipeline?", "Describe your experience with infrastructure as code."
      ii. Question 2: "Explain how you would design a scalable e-commerce platform using microservices."
         - Competency Tested: Technical Skills (System Design, Microservices)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Component breakdown, technology choices, scalability considerations.
         - Follow-up Probes: "How would you handle inter-service communication?", "What databases would you use for different microservices?", "How would you monitor and manage the microservices?"
      iii. Question 3: "Explain your approach to troubleshooting a production issue in a distributed system."
         - Competency Tested: Analytical Skills, Technical Skills (Troubleshooting, Distributed Systems)
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Systematic approach, tools used, escalation procedures.
         - Follow-up Probes: "What monitoring tools do you use?", "How do you identify the root cause of an issue?", "How do you prevent similar issues from recurring?"
   c. Behavioral Round:
      i. Question 1: "Describe a time when you had to learn a new technology quickly. What was your approach?"
         - Competency Tested: Adaptability, Learning Agility
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Resourcefulness, learning strategies, application of new knowledge.
         - Follow-up Probes: "What resources did you use to learn the new technology?", "How did you validate your understanding?", "How did you apply the new technology to solve a problem?"
      ii. Question 2: "Tell me about a time when you had to prioritize tasks under pressure. How did you decide what was most important?"
         - Competency Tested: Prioritization, Time Management
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Method for assessing priority, trade-offs made, outcomes.
         - Follow-up Probes: "What factors did you consider when prioritizing?", "How did you communicate your priorities to others?", "What was the outcome of your prioritization?"
      iii. Question 3: "Describe a situation where you had to work with a difficult team member. How did you handle it?"
         - Competency Tested: Communication Skills, Teamwork
         - Ideal Answer Indicators: Approach to conflict resolution, communication strategies, outcome.
         - Follow-up Probes: "How did you communicate with the difficult team member?", "What steps did you take to resolve the conflict?", "What was the impact on the team and project?"
4. Send Emails as and when needed:
   a. Send Interview Invitation Email
   b. Send Interview Reminder Email
   c. Send Thank You Email
5. Score Candidate Responses: (In task4)
   - Evaluate and score candidate responses against ideal answer benchmarks.
6. Generate Final Evaluation Report: (In task5)
   - Compile scores and interview feedback into a final evaluation report.
```
